rulings.Case Title: NEW HORIZON TRUST ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 24, 2025 line 1. RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. The Court has previously ruled on the Demurer to the original petition. The ruling stands. Specifically, the demurrer to petitioner's claims arising out of SB-35 has been and remains sustained without leave to amend. Petitioner chose not to proceed with the allowed amendment ("to amend for Plaintiff to state a claim regarding the purported violation of HAA and specify an application other than SB-35 application") in the next two iterations of the Complaint showing that no such amendment is possible. The demurrer to the cause of action for declaratory relief has been and remains OVERRULED. Therefore, at this time the demurrer to the first cause of action is sustained without leave to amend for reasons stated herein and in the order on the demurrer to the original petition; and the demurrer to the declaratory relief is OVERRULED. Respondent to file an answer within 10 days of notice of entry of order. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TROY SNOWDEN VS. S&S HOSPITALITY INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 24, 2025 line 3. PLAINTIFF TROY SNOWDEN DEMURRER TO AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. The Court has previously given the Cross-Complainant an opportunity to allege facts in support of the element of justifiable reliance on the Cross-Defendant's' misrepresentations. Cross-Complaint failed to so allege. Cross-Complainant did not allege any facts showing that the cross-complaint believed the representations or that such believe was reasonable. Instead, Cross-Complaint alleges facts showing that Cross-Complaint "would" or possibly did instead investigate the misrepresentation, i.e. did not believe or rely on them reasonably or otherwise instead Cross-Complaint proceeded to conduct discovery to rebut the misrepresentations. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: D.R. STEPHENS INDUSTRIAL PARTNERS, LLC, A VS. THERANOVA, L.L.C., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 24, 2025 line 4. PLAINTIFF D.R. STEPHENS INDUSTRIAL PARTNERS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY CORPORATION DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is overruled as to the cause of action for declaratory relief. This cause of action is sufficiently stated. Demurrer to the remaining causes of action is sustained with leave to amend for the Cross-Complainant to allege facts in support of each element of each cause of action, including but not limited to, (1) identifying the express provisions of the written contract that are breached; of there are no such express provisions in the contract, alleging any viable claim in light of the statute of frauds, parole evidence rule and integration clause and statute of limitations; (2) identifying independent wrongful acts; (3) identifying basis for rescission and restitution of all rents in light of the apparent allegation in the complaint that the benefits of the lease have been received by all parties from 2016 to 2023. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
